name: Manual and SMOTE Augmentation for Cross-Prompt ASAG
statement: Krisnawati, Mahastama, and Haw used manual response generation and SMOTE feature-space oversampling to supplement imbalanced short-answer grading data in a cross-prompt ASAG system.
population_or_context: Indonesian closed and semi-open short-answer grading with five grade categories and distinct training/testing prompts.
quantitative_note: Table V shows SMOTE improved some classifiers, including logistic regression MAF/WAF from 0.18/0.18 to 0.50/0.58 and kNN from 0.25/0.24 to 0.57/0.61, but did not improve linear regression.
evaluation_method: Cross-prompt evaluation using weighted average F1, macro average F1, and RMSE.
sparsity_or_imbalance_motivation: The study explicitly identifies multiclass imbalance in real-world ASAG data as a classifier challenge.
synthetic_data_research_role: Class-balancing and training-data supplementation for ASAG model development.
reported_metrics: Before and after SMOTE training sizes by prompt in Table III; classifier-level MAF/WAF/RMSE comparisons in Table V.
limitations: SMOTE samples are feature vectors rather than text responses.
Manual augmentation was produced by teaching assistants, not actual students.
source_locator: Full-text PDF; Abstract; Introduction; Research Method/Data Acquisition; Scoring Module; Results and Discussion; Tables II-V.
